perturbation.changed_text: 1.36 "Field" means any and all uses, except for uses related to pediatric cancers, which are explicitly excluded.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines "Field" as "any and all uses," meaning the collaboration covers all potential applications of the drug. The modified text introduces an exclusion for pediatric cancers. This directly contradicts the 'any and all uses' definition, creating ambiguity about the scope of the collaboration and creating an In-Text Contradiction. This is specifically contradicting the statement in Section 3.1(a), where it states RevMed is granting Sanofi to research, develop, manufacture use, sell, offer for sale, import and otherwise commercialize and exploit Products in the Field in the Licensed Territory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Licenses and Option to Sanofi. Licenses. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, RevMed hereby grants to Sanofi an exclusive (even as to RevMed and its Affiliates), royalty-bearing license (which shall be sub-licensable solely as provided in Section 3.4) under the RevMed Licensed Technology, to Research, Develop, Manufacture, use, sell, offer for sale, import and otherwise Commercialize and exploit Products (including, for clarity, any Companion Diagnostics with respect to such Products) in the Field in the Licensed Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Field" as "any and all uses," granting Sanofi a broad license as stated in Section 3.1. The changed text introduces an explicit exclusion for pediatric cancers, which directly contradicts the "any and all uses" definition and limits the scope of the license granted in Section 3.1. This creates a clear contradiction because Section 3.1 grants Sanofi the right to commercialize products "in the Field," but the modified definition of "Field" now excludes pediatric cancers, thus limiting Sanofi's rights. Therefore, the modification creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.75 "Person" means any individual, partnership, limited liability company, firm, corporation, association, trust, unincorporated organization or other entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.75 "Person" means any individual, partnership, limited liability company, firm, corporation, association, trust, unincorporated organization or other entity. However, for purposes of Article XIV (Indemnification; Liability; Insurance), "Person" specifically excludes government entities.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Person" is broad and inclusive. The modified text adds a carve-out for government entities *only* with respect to the indemnification clauses (Article XIV). This creates an in-text contradiction because government entities are initially included in the general definition of Person, but then are excluded from the concept of Person only when it comes to indemnification, which creates a contradiction to Section 14.1, as Sanofi is agreeing to hold RevMed's Person as not liable, but according to this perturbation, this is not the case anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.1 Indemnification by RevMed. RevMed shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Sanofi, its Affiliates and their respective officers, directors, agents and employees ("Sanofi Indemnitees") from and against any Third Party Claims and Losses arising therefrom under or related to this Agreement against any of them to the extent arising or resulting from: (a) the negligence, recklessness or willful misconduct of any of the RevMed Indemnitees; or (b) the material breach of any of the warranties or representations made by RevMed to Sanofi under this Agreement or any Ancillary Agreement; or (c) the material breach by RevMed of any of its obligations pursuant to this Agreement or any Ancillary Agreement; except in each case ((a) through (c)), to the extent the applicable Third Party Claim and Losses arising therefrom arise or result from (i) the negligence, recklessness or willful misconduct of any Sanofi Indemnitee; (ii) the breach of any of the warranties or representations made by Sanofi to RevMed under this Agreement or any Ancillary Agreement; or (iii) any breach by Sanofi of its obligations pursuant to this Agreement or any Ancillary Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Person' includes all entities, including government entities. Article 14.1 states that RevMed shall indemnify Sanofi for claims against 'Sanofi Indemnitees', which are defined as 'Sanofi, its Affiliates and their respective officers, directors, agents and employees'. If a government entity were a Sanofi Affiliate, the original definition would have RevMed indemnify Sanofi against any third-party claims. The modified text, however, excludes government entities from the definition of 'Person' *only* for Article XIV (Indemnification; Liability; Insurance). This creates a direct conflict because under the modified text, RevMed is no longer obligated to indemnify Sanofi if the claim arises from the actions of a government entity that is an affiliate of Sanofi. This directly contradicts the broadly worded indemnification clause in 14.1 which, under the original definition, would have included government entities.

perturbation.original_text: 1.125 "Third Party" means any Person other than a Party or an Affiliate of a Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.125 "Third Party" means any Person other than a Party or an Affiliate of a Party, unless explicitly stated otherwise in Section 5.3(b) where Third Party may refer to Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Third Party' is changed to include an exception that it *could* refer to Affiliates. This is contradictory to this initial definition. It creates uncertainty around the obligations related to Third Parties referenced elsewhere. This perturbation is directly contradicting Section 5.3, where it will be unclear whether the product development will the Third Party refers to, is an Affiliate or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
5.3 Combination Therapies. (a) The JRDC shall discuss whether to include in the Development Plan for a Product the Development of such Product for use with other products to the extent not already provided for in the Development Plan (each, a "Combination Therapy"), including products developed or sold by a Third Party or that are in the public domain. Subject to this Section 5.3, each Party shall have the right to propose to the JRDC studies for co-development of Products with other products under the applicable Development Plan.
(b) The Development Plan shall address the conduct of any Clinical Trial for a Combination Therapy and shall (i) specify which Party will be responsible for each activity for the Development of such Combination Therapy and (ii) specify which Party will be responsible for obtaining supplies of the Product or other product in such Combination Therapy as necessary. The JRDC shall review and approve the terms of any agreement with a Third Party in connection with any supply or other aspect of Development of such Combination Therapy.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Third Party" introduces a direct contradiction with Section 5.3. The original definition explicitly excludes Affiliates. The changed definition creates an exception, stating that in Section 5.3(b), "Third Party" *may* refer to Affiliates. This creates ambiguity in Section 5.3(b) regarding the JRDC's agreement with a "Third Party" as it's unclear whether this refers to affiliates or not, leading to conflicting obligations, therefore resulting in in-text contradiction, as per the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
